Important: FishLife values model estimates, direct measurements. Always verify retrieved values appropriate application, update local data possible. calling create_critter, check closest_taxa_match field returned object confirm FishLife matched intended taxon. recommend using scientific_name rather common_name. see error like Error seq.default(min_age, max_age, = time_step), typically network connectivity issue — simply re-running code usually resolves .

title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: start building fauna object using create_critter. arguments straightforward life history parameters. Two worth highlighting: adult_home_range recruit_home_range specify linear distance (km) within 95% adults recruits, respectively, expected disperse source patch course year.   fleets object list fishing fleets, created create_fleet. Every fleet contains one metier per species, specifying fleet’s interaction given critter: price per unit weight, selectivity, exploitation share. p_explt argument controls share total fishing mortality species attributed metier — values relative, absolute. two metiers p_explt = 1 p_explt = 2 species X, first accounts one-third total mortality second two-thirds.  tune_fleets calibrates catchability coefficients fleet achieves specified equilibrium depletion level. target 25% depletion, meaning fished biomass equals 25% unfished biomass equilibrium. calibration accounts fleet dynamics p_explt weights.  fauna fleets defined, pass simmar run simulation: process_marlin tidies raw simulation output data frames; plot_marlin provides quick visualisations:
code: fauna <-   list(     "bigeye" = create_critter(       common_name = "bigeye tuna",       adult_home_range = 5,       recruit_home_range = 15,       density_dependence = "local_habitat",       seasons = seasons,       depletion = .25,       resolution = resolution,       patch_area = patch_area,       steepness = 0.6,       ssb0 = 42,       m = 0.4     )   )  fauna$bigeye$plot() fauna$bigeye$plot_movement() fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list("bigeye" = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$bigeye,       price = 10,       sel_form = "logistic",       sel_start = 1,       sel_delta = .01,       catchability = 0,       p_explt = 1,     )),     base_effort = prod(resolution),     resolution = resolution   ) ) fleets$longline$metiers$bigeye$plot_selectivity() fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "explt")  fleets$longline$metiers$bigeye$plot_catchability() start_time <- Sys.time()  example_sim <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )  Sys.time() - start_time #> Time difference of 0.03742099 secs # options(warn = 2)  processed_marlin <- process_marlin(sim = example_sim, time_step = time_step)  plot_marlin(processed_marlin) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "c", max_scale = FALSE) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "n", plot_type = "length", fauna = fauna) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "ssb", plot_type = "space", steps_to_plot = max(processed_marlin$fauna$step))

what: Two species, two fleets, with seasonal habitat shifts
title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: example extends simulation two species two fleets, adding seasonal habitat dynamics species occupies different spatial distribution depending time year.
code: seasons <- 4  steps <- years * seasons  time_step <- 1 / seasons  skipjack_home_range <- 2  bigeye_home_range <- 5  # Construct habitat quality surfaces as [ny, nx] matrices  skipjack_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   dplyr::mutate(     habitat = dnorm((x^2 + y^2), 20, 200),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * skipjack_home_range   ) |>   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   bigeye_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm((x^2 + y^2), 300, 100),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * bigeye_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   bigeye_habitat2 <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm((x^.2 + y^.2), 100, 100),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * bigeye_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()  a <- Sys.time()  fauna <-   list(     "skipjack" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Katsuwonus pelamis",       habitat = list(skipjack_habitat, skipjack_habitat), # pass habitat as lists       season_blocks = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)), # seasons each habitat apply to       recruit_habitat = skipjack_habitat,       adult_home_range = skipjack_home_range, # standard deviation of the number of patches moved by adults       depletion = .6, # desired equilibrium depletion with fishing (1 = unfished, 0 = extinct),       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.2,       explt_type = "f"     ),     "bigeye" = create_critter(       common_name = "bigeye tuna",       habitat = list(bigeye_habitat, bigeye_habitat2), # pass habitat as lists       season_blocks = list(c(1, 2), c(3, 4)), # seasons each habitat apply to       recruit_habitat = bigeye_habitat,       adult_home_range = bigeye_home_range,       depletion = .1,       density_dependence = "local_habitat",       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.3,       explt_type = "f"     )   ) Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 1.394839 secs  # Each fleet contains one metier per species. # price = price per unit weight; sel_form = "logistic" or "dome"   fauna$skipjack$plot_movement() fauna$bigeye$plot_movement() fleets <- list(   "longline" = create_fleet(     list(       "skipjack" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$skipjack,         price = 100,         # price per unit weight         sel_form = "logistic",         # selectivity form, one of logistic or dome         sel_start = .3,         # percentage of length at maturity that selectivity starts         sel_delta = .1,         # additional percentage of sel_start where selectivity asymptotes         catchability = .01,         # overwritten by tune_fleet but can be set manually here         p_explt = 1       ),       "bigeye" = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 10,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = .1,         sel_delta = .01,         catchability = 0,         p_explt = 1       )     ),     base_effort = prod(resolution),     resolution = resolution   ),   "purseseine" = create_fleet(     list(       skipjack = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$skipjack,         price = 100,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = 0.25,         sel_delta = .1,         catchability = .01,         p_explt = 0.9       ),       bigeye = Metier$new(         critter = fauna$bigeye,         price = 100,         sel_form = "logistic",         sel_start = .25,         sel_delta = .5,         catchability = .01,         p_explt = 1       )     ),     base_effort = prod(resolution), resolution = resolution   ) )  a <- Sys.time()  fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets)  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.2995832 secs   # Run the simulation a <- Sys.time()  sim3 <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.07267809 secs # a <- Sys.time()  processed_marlin <- process_marlin(sim = sim3, time_step = time_step, keep_age = TRUE) # Sys.time() - a  plot_marlin(processed_marlin) plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "c") plot_marlin(processed_marlin, plot_var = "n", plot_type = "length", fauna = fauna)

title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: example compares effect identical MPA two species — yellowfin tuna shortfin mako shark — two spatial configurations. first scenario species share nearshore (eastern) habitat. second, tuna remain nearshore mako population centred offshore. MPA designed around tuna distribution cases, illustrating bycatch species’ unobserved range shift can determine whether MPA helps harms species.    define MPA data frame columns x, y, mpa, mpa = TRUE marks closed patches:  simulate MPA passing simmar via manager argument. mpa_year controls closure activated:  Now run offshore scenario: tuna habitat unchanged, mako population shifted offshore. apply exact MPA compare outcomes.
code: resolution <- c(20, 20) # resolution is in squared patches, so 20 implies a 20X20 system, i.e. 400 patches  seasons <- 1  years <- 100  tune_type <- "depletion"  steps <- years * seasons  yft_home_range <- 6  yft_depletion <- 0.6  mako_depletion <- 0.2  mako_home_range <- 5  yft_b0 <- 10000  mako_b0 <- 420  # Both species share the same nearshore (high-x) habitat in this first scenario  yft_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm(x, resolution, 8),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * yft_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   mako_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm(x, resolution, 8),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * mako_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   # Nearshore scenario: both species concentrated toward the eastern edge fauna <-   list(     "Yellowfin Tuna" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Thunnus albacares",       habitat = yft_habitat, # pass habitat as lists       recruit_habitat = yft_habitat,       adult_home_range = yft_home_range, # cells per year       depletion = yft_depletion, # desired equilibrium depletion with fishing (1 = unfished, 0 = extinct),       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       b0 = yft_b0     ),     "Shortfin Mako" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Isurus oxyrinchus",       habitat = list(mako_habitat), # pass habitat as lists       recruit_habitat = mako_habitat,       adult_home_range = mako_home_range,       depletion = mako_depletion,       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       burn_years = 200,       seasons = seasons,       fec_form = "pups",       pups = 6,       b0 = mako_b0,       lorenzen_m = TRUE,       lorenzen_c = -0.25     )   )  fauna$`Shortfin Mako`$plot() fleets <- list("longline" = create_fleet(   list(     `Yellowfin Tuna` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Yellowfin Tuna`,       price = 100, # price per unit weight       sel_form = "logistic", # selectivity form, one of logistic or dome       sel_start = .3, # percentage of length at maturity that selectivity starts       sel_delta = .1, # additional percentage of sel_start where selectivity asymptotes       catchability = .01, # overwritten by tune_fleet but can be set manually here       p_explt = 1     ),     `Shortfin Mako` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Shortfin Mako`,       price = 10,       sel_form = "logistic",       sel_start = .1,       sel_delta = .01,       catchability = 0,       p_explt = 1     )   ),   mpa_response = "stay",   base_effort = prod(resolution),   resolution = resolution,   spatial_allocation = "rpue",   cost_per_unit_effort = 1,   effort_cost_exponent = 1.3 ))  a <- Sys.time()  # before <- fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$catchability #  # fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$spatial_catchability   fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = tune_type, tune_costs = TRUE,years = floor(years * .5)) # tunes the catchability by fleet to achieve target depletion  # fleets$longline$base_effort # after =  fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$catchability   # fleets$longline$metiers[[1]]$catchability # fleets$longline$metiers[[1]]$catchability # fleets$longline$metiers[[2]]$catchability  # new_fleets$longline$base_effort # before == after  # fleets$longline$metiers$`Yellowfin Tuna`$spatial_catchability  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 1.943853 secs  # Run the nearshore baseline (no MPA) a <- Sys.time()  nearshore <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.2241461 secs  proc_nearshore <- process_marlin(nearshore, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step)  plot_marlin(proc_nearshore, max_scale = FALSE, plot_var = "b") plot_marlin(proc_nearshore, max_scale = FALSE, plot_var = "ssb") set.seed(42) # specify some MPA locations mpa_locations <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(mpa = x > 15 & y < 15)  mpa_locations %>%   ggplot(aes(x, y, fill = mpa)) +   geom_tile() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Accent", direction = -1, name = "MPA") +   scale_x_continuous(name = "Lon") +   scale_y_continuous(name = "Lat") a <- Sys.time()  nearshore_mpa <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years,   manager = list(mpas = list(     locations = mpa_locations,     mpa_year = floor(years * .5)   )) )  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.335737 secs  proc_nearshore_mpa <- process_marlin(nearshore_mpa, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step)  plot_marlin(proc_nearshore_mpa, max_scale = FALSE) mako_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = dnorm(x, .7 * resolution, 6),     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * mako_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   # Offshore scenario: same tuna habitat, mako now centred further from shore  fauna <-   list(     "Yellowfin Tuna" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Thunnus albacares",       habitat = yft_habitat, # pass habitat as lists       recruit_habitat = yft_habitat,       adult_home_range = yft_home_range, # cells per year       depletion = yft_depletion, # desired equilibrium depletion with fishing (1 = unfished, 0 = extinct),       density_dependence = "global_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       b0 = yft_b0     ),     "Shortfin Mako" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Isurus oxyrinchus",       habitat = list(mako_habitat), # what: De facto MPAs through bycatch penalties
title: Simulation of Marine Social-Ecological Systems
text: Fishing fleets can deterred areas without formal closures making certain patches unprofitable. example shows assigning large negative price shortfin mako — representing bycatch liability regulatory penalty — causes fleet avoid mako habitat, effectively creating de facto protected area.
code: years <- 100  tune_type <- "f"  # YFT habitat increases linearly with x (nearshore); mako is confined to the far corner (x>12, y>12)  yft_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = .05 * x,     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * yft_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   mako_habitat <- expand_grid(x = 1:resolution[1], y = 1:resolution[2]) %>%   mutate(     habitat = x > 12 & y > 12,     habitat = habitat / max(habitat) * mako_home_range   ) %>%   pivot_wider(names_from = x, values_from = habitat) %>%   select(-y) %>%   as.matrix()   # Build fauna for the bycatch penalty scenario  fauna <-   list(     "Yellowfin Tuna" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Thunnus albacares",       habitat = list(yft_habitat),       recruit_habitat = yft_habitat,       adult_home_range = yft_home_range,       depletion = .4,       density_dependence = "local_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.12,       explt_type = "f"     ),     "Shortfin Mako" = create_critter(       scientific_name = "Isurus oxyrinchus",       habitat = list(mako_habitat),       recruit_habitat = mako_habitat,       adult_home_range = mako_home_range,       depletion = .3,       density_dependence = "local_habitat", # recruitment form, where 1 implies local recruitment       burn_years = 200,       seasons = seasons,       init_explt = 0.1,       explt_type = "f"     )   )  # A large negative mako price makes patches where mako is present unprofitable, deterring effort  fleets <- list("longline" = create_fleet(   list(     `Yellowfin Tuna` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Yellowfin Tuna`,       price = 100, # price per unit weight       sel_form = "logistic", # selectivity form, one of logistic or dome       sel_start = .3, # percentage of length at maturity that selectivity starts       sel_delta = .1, # additional percentage of sel_start where selectivity asymptotes       catchability = .01, # overwritten by tune_fleet but can be set manually here       p_explt = 1     ),     `Shortfin Mako` = Metier$new(       critter = fauna$`Shortfin Mako`,       price = -20000,       sel_form = "logistic",       sel_start = .1,       sel_delta = .01,       catchability = 0,       p_explt = 1     )   ),   mpa_response = "stay",   base_effort = prod(resolution),   resolution = resolution ))  a <- Sys.time()  fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = tune_type) # tunes the catchability by fleet to achieve target depletion  Sys.time() - a #> Time difference of 0.1131761 secs  # Run the simulation negative_prices <- simmar(   fauna = fauna,   fleets = fleets,   years = years )    proc_negative_prices <- process_marlin(negative_prices, time_step = fauna[[1]]$time_step) plot_marlin(   `De-Facto MPA` = proc_negative_prices,   plot_var = "ssb",   plot_type = "space" )

title: Allocate Catches, Revenue, and Profit by Fleet — allocate_yields
text: fishing-mortality share fleet patch-age cell given f_p_a_fl / f_p_a (already normalised). Total catch cell multiplied share give fleet-specific catch. Revenue calculated catch times price (p_p_a_fl). Profit revenue minus patch-level cost, costs follow formula: $$C_{l,p} = c_{0,l} \, E^{ref}_l \left[\left(\frac{E_{l,p}/n_{sp}}{E^{ref}_l}\right)^{\gamma_l} + \theta_l \, \tilde{d}_{l,p} \, \frac{E_{l,p}/n_{sp}}{E^{ref}_l}\right]$$ Note effort divided number species (\(n_{sp}\)) avoid double-counting costs across species loop simmar. internal helper called per critter per time step inside simmar go_fish.

what: Calculate Marginal Revenue and Marginal Profit by Patch and Fleet — calc_marginal_value
title: Calculate Marginal Revenue and Marginal Profit by Patch and Fleet — calc_marginal_value
text: Uses forward finite differences go_fish approximate \(\partial R / \partial E_{p,f}\) (marginal revenue) \(\partial \Pi / \partial E_{p,f}\) (marginal profit) every patch-fleet combination. output matrices designed merged directly buffet list consumed allocate_effort fleet's spatial_allocation "marginal_revenue" "marginal_profit", fleet_model = "sole_owner".

title: Calculate Marginal Revenue and Marginal Profit by Patch and Fleet — calc_marginal_value
code: calc_marginal_value(   e_p_fl,   fauna,   n_p_a,   fleets,   baseline = NULL,   method = c("separable", "patch_loop"),   epsilon = 0.001 )

title: Calculate Marginal Revenue and Marginal Profit by Patch and Fleet — calc_marginal_value
text: e_p_fl Numeric matrix effort patch (rows) fleet (columns). fauna Named list fauna objects (go_fish). n_p_a Named list numbers--age matrices patch (one element per species), representing current population state. fleets Named list fleet objects (go_fish). baseline Optional. List returned prior call go_fish(output_format = "matrix") inputs. Supplying avoids one redundant baseline evaluation can improve performance marginals computed alongside existing buffet. method Character. Approximation strategy; see Details. One "separable" (default) "patch_loop". epsilon Numeric. Additive effort perturbation finite difference. small relative typical effort values large enough avoid floating-point noise. Default 1e-3.

title: Calculate Marginal Revenue and Marginal Profit by Patch and Fleet — calc_marginal_value
text: named list two elements, patches x fleets matrix dimension names e_p_fl: mr_p_fl Marginal revenue \(\partial R / \partial E_{p,f}\) patch fleet. mp_p_fl Marginal profit \(\partial \Pi / \partial E_{p,f}\) patch fleet. Append buffet go_fish buffet$mr_p_fl buffet$mp_p_fl use allocate_effort.

title: Calculate Marginal Revenue and Marginal Profit by Patch and Fleet — calc_marginal_value
text: Two approximation strategies available: "separable" Bumps effort patches one fleet simultaneously epsilon, computes derivatives single extra go_fish call per fleet. Requires \(n_{fleets}\) extra evaluations. Fast, assumes marginal return patch \(\) depend perturbation patch \(j\) (valid per-patch depletion effects negligible, typical large grids). "patch_loop" Bumps effort one patch time, requiring \(n_{patches} \times n_{fleets}\) extra evaluations. Fully accounts cross-patch depletion effects; appropriate small, dense grids within-step movements significant. Called automatically simmar fleet uses marginal allocation sole-owner model. results appended buffet mr_p_fl mp_p_fl.

title: Calculate Marginal Revenue and Marginal Profit by Patch and Fleet — calc_marginal_value
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Compute baseline buffet buffet <- go_fish(e_p_fl, fauna, n_p_a, fleets, output_format = "matrix")  # Append marginal values (reuse baseline to avoid redundant evaluation) marginals <- calc_marginal_value(   e_p_fl   = e_p_fl,   fauna    = fauna,   n_p_a    = n_p_a,   fleets   = fleets,   baseline = buffet,   method   = "separable" )  buffet$mr_p_fl <- marginals$mr_p_fl buffet$mp_p_fl <- marginals$mp_p_fl  # Now allocate effort using marginal profit result <- allocate_effort(   effort_by_patch = e_p_fl,   buffet          = buffet,   fleets          = fleets,   open_patch      = open_patches ) } # }

code: create_critter(   common_name = NA,   scientific_name = NA,   get_common_name = FALSE,   critter_type = "fish",   habitat = list(),   season_blocks = list(),   recruit_habitat = NA,   seasons = 1,   lorenzen_c = -1,   fec_form = "weight",   adult_home_range = 1,   recruit_home_range = 2,   adult_diffusion = NULL,   recruit_diffusion = NULL,   depletion = 0.4,   fished_depletion = NULL,   init_explt = 0.1,   f = NULL,   explt_type = "f",   burn_years = 50,   weight_a = NA,   fec_expo = 1,   resolution = c(10, 10),   patch_area = 1,   spawning_seasons = NA,   density_dependence = "global_habitat",   steepness = 0.8,   growth_model = "von_bertalanffy",   ... )

title: Create a Critter (Species) Object — create_critter
text: common_name Character. Common name species (e.g. "yellowfin tuna"). Used look life history FishLife scientific_name supplied. scientific_name Character. Scientific name (e.g. "Thunnus albacares"). Preferred common_name FishLife lookup; case-insensitive. get_common_name Logical. TRUE, resolves common name scientific_name via internet lookup. Default FALSE. critter_type Character. Currently "fish" supported. Placeholder future non-fish implementations. habitat List habitat-quality matrices (one per season block). matrix must [ny, nx]. Values represent relative habitat quality log scale; drive movement taxis , depending density_dependence, recruit distribution. Generate sim_habitat. season_blocks List integer vectors specifying seasons belong habitat block (e.g. list(1:2, 3:4) two-block, four-season year). Must align habitat. recruit_habitat Matrix NA. Habitat matrix used distribute new recruits. Defaults adult habitat first season block NA. seasons Integer. Number seasons per year. Sets time_step = 1 / seasons. critters fauna list must share value. lorenzen_c Numeric. Exponent Lorenzen (1996) size-dependent natural mortality function. Negative values (default -1) give higher mortality smaller fish. fec_form Character. Fecundity form: "weight" (fecundity proportional body weight; default) "pups" (fixed litter size, e.g. elasmobranchs). adult_home_range Numeric NULL. Characteristic adult home range (km). NULL, used derive adult diffusion coefficient takes precedence adult_diffusion. Set NULL use adult_diffusion directly. recruit_home_range Numeric NULL. Characteristic recruit home range (km). NULL, used derive recruit diffusion coefficient takes precedence recruit_diffusion. Set NULL use recruit_diffusion directly. adult_diffusion Numeric NULL. Adult diffusion rate (km^2 per time step). Used adult_home_range NULL. recruit_diffusion Numeric NULL. Recruit diffusion rate. Used recruit_home_range NULL. depletion Numeric (0, 1]. Target spawning stock biomass relative unfished SSB (B/B0) start simulation. fished_depletion Numeric. Alias depletion; takes precedence supplied. init_explt Numeric. Initial annual exploitation rate (fraction exploitable population removed per year). f Numeric. Alias init_explt. explt_type Character. One "f" (instantaneous fishing mortality; default) "fmsy" (multiple Fmsy). burn_years Integer. Number years burn unfished population equilibrium starting simulation. Default 50. weight_a Numeric NA. Intercept alpha allometric weight--length relationship \(W = \alpha L^\beta\). Looked FishLife NA. fec_expo Numeric. Exponent fecundity-weight relationship. Values > 1 produce hyperallometric fecundity. Default 1. resolution Integer scalar length-2 integer vector c(nx, ny) giving grid dimensions. scalar replicated square grid. patch_area Numeric. Area patch (km^2). Used scale diffusion compute density-dependent quantities. spawning_seasons Integer vector. seasons spawning occurs . Defaults seasons NA. density_dependence Character. Density dependence form; see Details. One "global_habitat", "local_habitat", "pre_dispersal", "post_dispersal", "global_ssb". steepness Numeric (0.2, 1). Beverton-Holt steepness parameter \(h\) governing shape spawner-recruit relationship. growth_model Character. Growth model length--age: "von_bertalanffy" (default), "power", "growth_cessation". ... Additional parameters forwarded Fish R6 class constructor. Common options include k, linf, t0, m, age_mature, weight_b, ssb0, sigma_rec (recruitment standard deviation), ac_rec (recruitment autocorrelation). See ?Fish full list.

title: Create a Critter (Species) Object — create_critter

title: Create a Critter (Species) Object — create_critter
text: "global_habitat" Beverton-Holt spawner-recruit using global SSB; recruits distributed proportionally habitat. "local_habitat" Recruitment density dependence acts locally within patch. "pre_dispersal" Competition occurs larval dispersal. "post_dispersal" Competition occurs larval dispersal. "global_ssb" Beverton-Holt using global SSB uniform spatial recruit distribution.

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet
code: create_fleet(   metiers,   mpa_response = "stay",   fleet_model = "constant_effort",   oa_max_growth_per_year = 0.5,   oa_max_decline_per_year = 0.5,   oa_signal_half = 0.3,   cost_per_unit_effort = 1,   spatial_allocation = "rpue",   effort_cost_exponent = 1.2,   travel_fraction = 0,   ports = NULL,   cost_per_distance = 1,   cr_ratio = 1,   resolution,   patch_area = 1,   base_effort = NULL,   fishing_grounds = NULL,   eta = 0.05 )

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet
text: metiers Named list Metier R6 objects, one per species fauna. metier specifies price, selectivity, relative catchability fleet-species combination. Names must match species names fauna. mpa_response Character. Vessel response MPA closures: "stay" (vessels stay closed area) "leave" (vessels redistribute open patches, concentrating effort). fleet_model Character. Effort dynamics model; see Details. One "constant_effort" (default), "open_access", "sole_owner", "manual". oa_max_growth_per_year Numeric. Maximum fractional increase total effort per year highly profitable conditions open-access sole-owner fleets (e.g. 0.5 = +50% per year). Converted per-step multiplier internally. Must > 0. oa_max_decline_per_year Numeric (0, 1). Maximum fractional decrease total effort per year highly unprofitable conditions (e.g. 0.5 = -50% per year). Must (0, 1). oa_signal_half Numeric (0, 1). Profitability signal value effort adjustment speed reaches half maximum. Lower values make fleets sensitive. Typical range 0.15–0.4. Default 0.3. cost_per_unit_effort Numeric. Base cost per unit effort. Overridden tune_fleets tune_costs = TRUE; rarely needs manual adjustment. spatial_allocation Character. Spatial effort allocation strategy; see Details. Default "rpue". effort_cost_exponent Numeric. Exponent \(\gamma\) effort cost function, controlling congestion / convexity. Values > 1 make additional units effort progressively expensive. Default 1.2. travel_fraction Numeric [0, 1). Fraction total costs attributable travel equilibrium. 0 means spatial cost heterogeneity (patches equally costly). Controls strongly port proximity shapes spatial cost surface. ports Data frame columns x y giving port patch coordinates. Minimum distances patch nearest port used compute travel-cost component travel_fraction > 0. cost_per_distance Numeric. Deprecated; use travel_fraction instead. cr_ratio Numeric. Target cost--revenue ratio equilibrium. 1 implies zero profits (open-access equilibrium). Used tune_fleets calibrate cost_per_unit_effort. resolution Integer scalar length-2 integer vector c(nx, ny). Must match resolution fauna objects. patch_area Numeric. Area patch (km^2). Used compute port distances. base_effort Numeric. Total effort units available fleet. Defaults prod(resolution) (one unit per patch). Catchability calibrated relative value tune_fleets. fishing_grounds Data frame columns x, y, fishing_ground (logical numeric). Restricts effort can deployed; TRUE/1 = open, FALSE/0 = closed. spatial_allocation = "manual", numeric values fishing_ground used effort weights. Defaults patches open. eta Numeric. Internal responsiveness scaling parameter allocate_effort. Default 0.1.

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet
text: named list (fleet object) parameters needed simmar tune_fleets, including computed travel weights, normalised cost-per-patch, (open-access / sole-owner fleets) derived annual effort-adjustment parameters.

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet
text: "constant_effort" Total effort fixed base_effort time step. Use scenarios fishing pressure prescribed externally. "open_access" Total effort adjusts step based normalised average-profitability signal. Equilibrates total profits = 0. Entry/exit speed controlled oa_max_growth_per_year, oa_max_decline_per_year, oa_signal_half. "sole_owner" Identical dynamics "open_access" uses marginal (average) profit signal. Equilibrates MEY marginal profit = 0. Requires calc_marginal_value() computed step; simmar handles automatically. "manual" Total effort step taken directly user-supplied vector; see manager argument simmar.

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet
text: spatial_allocation argument determines total fleet effort distributed among patches step. Options: "rpue" Revenue per unit effort (default). Effort concentrates high-revenue patches. "revenue" Total revenue. Similar "rpue" favours larger patches. "ppue" Profit per unit effort (cost-aware). "profit" Total profit (cost-aware). "cpue" / "catch" Catch-based variants. "marginal_revenue" / "marginal_profit" Uses finite- difference marginal returns calc_marginal_value. Required "sole_owner" fleets. Requires fleet_model = "sole_owner" explicit pre-computation. "manual" Effort distributed proportionally continuous weights fishing_grounds$fishing_ground (0–1 valued). "uniform" Effort spread equally across open patches.

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet
text: Total cost per fleet : $$C = c_0 \, E^{ref} \sum_l \left[\left(\frac{E_l}{E^{ref}}\right)^\gamma + \theta \, \tilde{d}_l \frac{E_l}{E^{ref}}\right]$$ \(c_0\) cost_per_unit_effort, \(E^{ref}\) reference effort per patch, \(\gamma\) effort_cost_exponent, \(\theta\) travel_weight (derived travel_fraction), \(\tilde{d}_l\) normalised distance patch \(l\) nearest port.

title: Create a Fleet Object — create_fleet
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Create a metier for a single species met <- Metier$new(   critter     = fauna[["tuna"]],   price       = 10,   sel_form    = "logistic",   sel_start   = 0.3,   sel_delta   = 0.1,   catchability = 0.01,   p_explt     = 1 )  # Constant-effort fleet fleet <- create_fleet(   metiers    = list(tuna = met),   resolution = c(10, 10) )  # Open-access fleet with port-based travel costs ports <- data.frame(x = 1, y = 1) oa_fleet <- create_fleet(   metiers              = list(tuna = met),   fleet_model          = "open_access",   spatial_allocation   = "ppue",   travel_fraction      = 0.3,   ports                = ports,   cr_ratio             = 0.9,   resolution           = c(10, 10) )  fleets <- list(fleet = fleet) fleets <- tune_fleets(fauna, fleets, tune_type = "depletion") } # }

what: Iteratively Optimise an MPA Network — optimize_mpa
title: Iteratively Optimise an MPA Network — optimize_mpa
text: Uses stochastic importance-resampling (SIR) algorithm identify MPA network maximises weighted combination biodiversity economic objectives. iteration function evaluates marginal value toggling individual patches MPA, expands contracts network patch(es) highest marginal objective value.

title: Iteratively Optimise an MPA Network — optimize_mpa
code: optimize_mpa(   fauna,   fleets,   starting_conditions = NA,   alpha = 0.33,   max_prop_mpa = 1,   resolution,   prop_sampled = 0.2,   max_delta = 2,   workers = 6,   bio_objective = "max_ssb",   econ_objective = "yield",   work_backwards = TRUE,   patches_at_a_time = 1 )

title: Iteratively Optimise an MPA Network — optimize_mpa
text: fauna Named list fauna objects create_critter. fleets Named list fleet objects create_fleet, tuned tune_fleets. starting_conditions Initial population state, typically sim[[length(sim)]] prior simmar run. Passed initial_conditions inner simmar call. alpha Numeric [0, 1]. Weight biodiversity (SSB/SSB0) composite objective. alpha = 1 ignores economics; alpha = 0 ignores biology. Default 0.33. max_prop_mpa Numeric (0, 1]. Maximum proportion patches can designated MPA. Setting 1 forces work_backwards = FALSE automatically. Default 1. resolution Integer scalar length-2 vector c(nx, ny). Must match resolution fauna fleets. prop_sampled Numeric (0, 1]. Proportion candidate patches evaluate iteration. Lower values speed computation increase stochasticity. Default 0.2. max_delta Numeric. Unused; kept backwards compatibility. workers Integer. Number parallel workers future::multisession. Default 6. bio_objective Character. Biodiversity sub-objective: "max_ssb" (default) Sum SSB/SSB0 across species; favours absolute biodiversity gains. "min_loss" Count species decline relative baseline; favours preventing losses. econ_objective Character. Economic sub-objective used evaluate candidate patch: "yield" (default) Total catch across species. "revenue" Total revenue. "profits" Total profit. work_backwards Logical. TRUE (default recommended), start full MPA coverage iteratively remove patches. Set FALSE grow network empty. patches_at_a_time Integer. Number patches toggle per iteration. Default 1.

title: Iteratively Optimise an MPA Network — optimize_mpa
text: named list: outcomes Tibble biodiversity economic outcomes MPA size level. objective Tibble composite objective value (obj) MPA size, plus component scores (ssb, econ, yield). mpa_network Tibble mapping iteration (proportion protected) corresponding MPA location data frame.

title: Iteratively Optimise an MPA Network — optimize_mpa
text: work_backwards = TRUE (recommended), algorithm starts 100\ improves weighted objective. "reverse greedy" approach tends produce stable networks forward greedy (start empty, add patches) avoids early lock-suboptimal configurations. composite objective iteration : $$obj = \alpha \cdot \tilde{B} + (1 - \alpha) \cdot \tilde{E}$$ \(\tilde{B}\) \(\tilde{E}\) percent-rank-scaled biodiversity economic outcomes, respectively. Scaling percent ranks means \(\alpha\) strictly controls weight given biodiversity relative economic rank, absolute magnitudes. Parallel evaluation candidate patches uses furrr::future_map; set workers control number parallel sessions.

what: Simulate Spatially Correlated Species Habitat — sim_habitat
title: Simulate Spatially Correlated Species Habitat — sim_habitat
text: Generates set spatially correlated habitat-quality surfaces one species using Matern covariance function (following Thorson & Barnett, 2017). Habitat values can positively negatively correlated across species, reflecting ecological affinities competitive exclusion. resulting matrices primary input habitat argument create_critter.

title: Simulate Spatially Correlated Species Habitat — sim_habitat
code: sim_habitat(   critters,   kp,   critter_correlations = NA,   resolution,   patch_area,   rescale_habitat = TRUE,   max_delta = 3,   max_abs_cor = 1,   min_abs_cor = 0,   output = "df" )

title: Simulate Spatially Correlated Species Habitat — sim_habitat
text: critters Character vector species names (length \(n\)). Names carried output list. kp Positive numeric. Matern spatial range parameter. Lower values produce smoother, correlated spatial distributions; higher values produce patchier maps. Typical values: 0.05–0.5. critter_correlations \(n \times n\) numeric correlation matrix specifying inter-species correlations habitat quality, NA (default) draw random correlations. Diagonal must 1; -diagonal entries must (-1, 1). resolution Integer scalar length-2 integer vector c(nx, ny) giving grid dimensions. Matches resolution argument create_critter. patch_area Numeric. Area patch (km^2). Used compute inter-patch distances km. rescale_habitat Logical. TRUE (default), rescales habitat values [0, log(max_delta)]. log scale means values drive movement taxis multiplicatively. max_delta Positive numeric. Upper bound habitat rescaling; passed rescale. Default 3. max_abs_cor Numeric (0, 1]. Maximum absolute value randomly generated inter-species correlations. Must exceed min_abs_cor. min_abs_cor Numeric [0, 1). Minimum absolute value randomly generated inter-species correlations. Default 0. output Character. One "df" (default) "list". "df" Returns data frame columns critter, patch, x, y, habitat. "list" Returns named list (one entry per species) [ny, nx] habitat matrices suitable direct use habitat argument create_critter.

title: Simulate Spatially Correlated Species Habitat — sim_habitat
text: list three elements: critter_distributions Either long data frame (output = "df") named list [ny, nx] habitat matrices (output = "list"). critter_correlations \(n \times n\) correlation matrix realised inter-species habitat correlations. wtf input generated correlation matrix (nearest positive-definite projection).

title: Simulate Spatially Correlated Species Habitat — sim_habitat
text: Spatial correlation among patches follows Matern covariance smoothness \(n = 1\). rate parameter kp controls spatial decorrelation length: value 0.1 produces broad, smooth habitat gradients, 1.0 produces patchy, rapidly decorrelating maps. Cross-species correlations drawn uniformly \([-max\_abs\_cor, -min\_abs\_cor] \cup [min\_abs\_cor, max\_abs\_cor]\) critter_correlations = NA. Supply full \(n \times n\) correlation matrix fix species correlations exactly. output = "list", habitat matrices returned [ny, nx] format column names 1:nx row names 1:ny, matching convention expected create_critter.

title: Simulate Spatially Correlated Species Habitat — sim_habitat
text: Thorson, J.T. & Barnett, L..K. (2017). Comparing estimates abundance trends distribution shifts using single- multispecies models fishes biogenic habitat. ICES Journal Marine Science, 74(5), 1311–1321. doi:10.1093/icesjms/fsw193

title: Run the marlin Spatially Explicit Fisheries Simulation — simmar
text: fauna Named list critter objects create_critter. critters must share time_step (.e. number seasons). fleets Named list fleet objects create_fleet, tuned tune_fleets. manager Named list management actions. Supported elements: mpas List $locations (data frame columns x, y, mpa; mpa = TRUE = closed) $mpa_year (integer year closures activated). quotas Named numeric vector annual catch caps per species (names match fauna). Effort scaled optimised multiplier cap exceeded. effort_cap Named numeric vector capping total effort per fleet open-access / sole-owner models. closed_seasons Named list integer vectors specifying seasons closed fishing critter. habitat Named list (one entry per critter) time-varying habitat. entry list [ny, nx] matrices (one per year per time step). provided, movement matrix updated step based difference habitat quality adjacent patches. years Numeric. Number years simulate. Ignored steps provided. steps Integer. Alternative years: number time steps simulate. Takes precedence years NA. starting_season Integer. Starting season within year. Rarely needed; used chaining partial-year runs. initial_conditions Initial population state, typically sim[[length(sim)]] previous simmar call. Defaults unfished equilibrium embedded critter object. starting_step Character. Step name form "year_season" start . Controls step labelling chaining runs. keep_starting_step Logical. TRUE (default), starting (initial-conditions) step included output. log_rec_devs Numeric matrix pre-generated log recruitment deviates rows = steps columns = critters (names must match fauna). Default diag(length(fauna)) (independent recruitment).

Pass output process_marlin tidying.

title: Run the marlin Spatially Explicit Fisheries Simulation — simmar
text: Effort dynamics: fleet, total effort step determined fleet model ("constant_effort", "open_access", "sole_owner", "manual"). Open-access sole-owner fleets use tanh-based normalised profitability signal derived previous step's revenues costs. Spatial allocation: allocate_effort redistributes fleet's total effort across patches using multiplicative velocity-field update driven chosen spatial_allocation metric ("rpue", "profit", etc.) previous step's buffet. Fishing & population dynamics: critter, age-structured fishing mortality \(F_{p,}\) applied, followed natural mortality, movement, Beverton-Holt recruitment. Yield accounting: allocate_yields aggregate_yields build per-fleet buffet catch, revenue, profit, CPUE patch. Marginal values (needed): fleet uses spatial_allocation = "marginal_profit" "marginal_revenue", fleet_model = "sole_owner", calc_marginal_value appends patch-level marginal returns buffet. Quota enforcement: manager$quotas set species, scalar effort multiplier solved via optim keep total catch quota.

title: Run the marlin Spatially Explicit Fisheries Simulation — simmar

code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Basic run sim <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets, years = 50)  # With MPA starting in year 10 mpa_locs <- expand_grid(x = 1:10, y = 1:10) |>   mutate(mpa = x <= 5) sim_mpa <- simmar(   fauna   = fauna,   fleets  = fleets,   years   = 50,   manager = list(     mpas = list(locations = mpa_locs, mpa_year = 10)   ) )  # Chain: baseline + policy scenario sim_base    <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets, years = 50) sim_policy  <- simmar(fauna = fauna, fleets = fleets, years = 20,                       initial_conditions = sim_base[[length(sim_base)]],                       manager = list(quotas = list(tuna = 500))) } # }
